curated.name: rolling_window_features_need_placebo_market
curated.content: Any knowledge-derived feature that is a LONG ROLLING WINDOW (corr63, long EWMA, etc.) injects mechanical autocorrelation (corr63: 0.94 at lag 5 on pure i.i.d. noise) and can manufacture a completeness PASS that year-shuffle noise twins miss (they preserve the rolling smoothness). Such features MUST pass the i.i.d. placebo-market guard, and no near-persistent rolling statistic may enter both the context and the target. Use within-block (no cross-block window) constructions.

corpus_priors.name: profitability_is_a_pipeline_property
corpus_priors.content: 52 years of evidence do not support one universal 'most profitable' model at the center of a bot; durable systems arise when a weak, regime-dependent, fast-decaying signal passes through causally correct data, hard temporal validation, cost-aware allocation, an independent risk veto, realistic execution, and reproducible operations. The three eras (1975-92, 1993-2010, 2011-26) are not three truths but three successive tightenings of the plausibility criterion. Profitability is a property of the whole signal-to-realized-PnL transformation, not of the predictor.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Would be revised only by multi-regime live proof that an off-the-shelf model core durably beats modular stacks net of costs; the corpus explicitly says no such evidence exists yet.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Baseline-ladder gate, computable from the daily panel: any challenger must beat BOTH (a) 1/N equal-weight buy-and-hold of the Dow-30 panel and (b) the current certified frozen rule, net of the 30bp/change cost budget with rf-accruing cash leg, on the same walk-forward splits. Promotion metric = net PnL difference, never forecast accuracy.

corpus_priors.name: search_freedom_manufactures_false_alpha
corpus_priors.content: Unaccounted search freedom makes the 'best found' strategy a selection artifact (White Reality Check; Bailey/Lopez de Prado PBO). Diagnostic indicators: sharp in-sample vs honest-OOS gap, unstable feature importances, sensitivity to the split date. The remedy is a trial ledger, frozen holdouts, and search-penalized thresholds; it breaks when informal post-hoc redesign continues after the 'final' test.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Breaks when candidates are redesigned after seeing the holdout, or when the trial count N is undercounted (shared-idea variants counted as one trial).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Three proxies from the daily panel plus the bot's own trial ledger: (1) IS/OOS gap = Sharpe_IS - Sharpe_OOS on temporal splits of the panel; (2) split-sensitivity = std of OOS Sharpe across K>=4 shifted split dates; (3) acceptance z-threshold deflated by ln(N_trials) from the experiment ledger (the project's frozen gate already implements this).

corpus_priors.name: optimizer_amplifies_estimation_error
corpus_priors.content: Noisy expected returns and covariances are amplified, not absorbed, by unconstrained optimizers; DeMiguel-Garlappi-Uppal show 14 sample-based schemes fail to beat 1/N stably out of sample, and Jagannathan-Ma show constraints act as shrinkage. Indicators: weight flips, leverage spikes, turnover explosions. The allocator must be an absorber of error, not an amplifier of conviction.
corpus_priors.horizon: every rebalance (days) and every allocation-design decision
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Breaks when the allocator trusts point estimates more than their error bars; also note the project's own P-1 result — even 1/N-style diversified fans lost to the certified concentrated chord on 2019-23, so 1/N is a floor, not a ceiling.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: From the bot's own weight vector on the daily panel: (1) turnover = 0.5*sum|w_t - w_(t-1)| per day, alarm on explosion vs its 63d median; (2) weight-instability = mean daily L1 distance of weights; (3) mandatory 1/N equal-weight benchmark leg run net-of-cost on the same dates; any optimizer output is shrunk toward 1/N with hard per-name caps.

corpus_priors.name: volume_is_not_absorbing_liquidity
corpus_priors.content: The SEC/CFTC Flash Crash report states directly that in significant volatility high trading volume is not a reliable indicator of liquidity; volume-as-liquidity and touch-equals-fill are named among the corpus's most repeated false-alpha channels. Automation can improve matching efficiency while worsening the visibility of true liquidity (BIS).
corpus_priors.horizon: days (measured daily), matters most in stress weeks
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The daily proxy is an approximation: Amihud from close*volume misses intraday depth withdrawal; treat it as a warning flag, never as a fill guarantee.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Per-ticker daily proxies from OHLCV: (1) Amihud illiquidity = 21d mean(|ret_d| / (close_d*volume_d)); (2) Corwin-Schultz high-low spread estimator from consecutive-day high/low; (3) liquidity-withdrawal flag = volume z-score > 2 AND Amihud z-score > 2 simultaneously (volume spike that does NOT absorb price impact). Down-size or refuse trades on flagged names/days.

corpus_priors.name: alpha_half_life_vs_cost_clock
corpus_priors.content: Short alpha half-life combined with positive costs makes any static target economically false: PnL that exists at mid/close evaporates under realistic friction, and urgency raises the cost further. A production signal must ship with an explicit half-life and the holding horizon must be consistent with it; a high-turnover strategy without its own execution/cost model is undermodeled regardless of its statistical quality.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Breaks when persistence is overestimated in-sample or impact is treated ex post; locally confirmed by the project's csi500 reversal being real-but-priced-out at 5d.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Horizon-IC curve from the daily panel: rank-IC(h) of the signal vs forward returns at h in {1,5,10,21}d; half-life = smallest h where IC(h) <= 0.5*IC(1). Gates: rebalance period >= half-life; turnover-to-edge ratio = (annual turnover * 30bp cost) / gross annual edge, kill if > 0.5; re-run PnL at 1x/2x/3x cost.

corpus_priors.name: state_before_direction_regime_gating
corpus_priors.content: The corpus's medium-confidence claim (deliberately weakened from the earlier 'state estimation is always more important'): state variables are often more useful as a control plane under frictions than as directional forecasts, and signals should be gated by volatility/liquidity regime with capital permission tied to regime. This matches the project's only certified real-data result (P(bear)-gated exposure dial).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Regime-dependent by construction: cointegration/stat-arb, trend overlays, and vol-gating are listed as context-dependent, not durable knowledge; a gate certified in one regime mix has no claim on unseen mixes.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Three daily state channels: (1) the existing 2-state regime belief P(bear); (2) vol state = 21d realized vol of the equal-weight Dow-30 daily return vs its rolling 252d median (binary); (3) liquidity state = cross-sectional median 21d Amihud vs its 252d rolling median. Signals carry a certified-regime tag and receive capital permission only inside their certified cell.

corpus_priors.name: uncertainty_aware_sizing_with_no_trade_band
corpus_priors.content: The mature forecast object is not next-bar sign but expected NET utility of an action at a known horizon and cost; a production signal must be a package of score + confidence + half-life + tradability, and weights must see confidence intervals, turnover budgets, and no-trade regions, not point forecasts. Uncertainty itself must enter the decision rule ex ante.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Breaks when confidence is estimated on too-short windows (noisy IC-IR whipsaws sizing) or when the no-trade band is tuned post hoc on the holdout.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Per signal from the daily panel: confidence = IC-IR = rolling 252d mean(rank-IC) / std(rank-IC); position delta executed only if |target_w - current_w| > no-trade band (band >= 2*assumed cost / expected edge per unit weight); size proportional to clip(IC-IR, 0, cap) * score under hard exposure caps.

corpus_priors.name: one_oos_band_is_not_proof
corpus_priors.content: Promoting a strategy on one attractive OOS stripe is a named false-alpha channel; honest validation requires rolling walk-forward splits, regime partitions, cost/capacity perturbation, and a shadow bridge before capital. OOS-as-proof is listed among fragile heuristics alongside accuracy-equals-PnL.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Breaks when the regime partition is itself chosen after seeing results (the project's own eval-grid phase-selection false-GREENs are exactly this failure, twice).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Promotion matrix computable from panels + free series: evaluate every candidate on K>=4 rolling walk-forward splits x 2 regime partitions (days with VIX above/below its rolling 252d median), all net-of-cost at 1x/2x/3x the 30bp budget; promote on the MINIMUM cell, never the mean or max; then a mandatory paper/shadow period before capital.

corpus_priors.name: independent_risk_veto_and_safe_flat
corpus_priors.content: Risk is an independent authority with the technical power to say no — cut aggressiveness, block orders, flatten — not a variance dashboard (analogized to Rule 15c3-5 pre-trade controls). Operational ambiguity destroys financial truth: stale feeds, silent history healing, unmarked asynchronous merges, and reconciliation breaks make the model learn ETL artifacts; when system state is ambiguous the correct position is safe-flat.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Breaks when the risk gate is decorative (no wired kill path) or when data checks are skipped for 'known good' feeds — the project's E-10 mixed-clock csi500 panel (close=yesterday, high/low=today) is a local proof of the silent-corruption mechanism.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Daily computable pre-trade checks: (1) freshness lock — if max(panel date) < last exchange trading day, exposure := 0; (2) per-row OHLC integrity — high >= max(open,close) and low <= min(open,close), quarantine violators; (3) cross-source clock check — sampled panel closes vs Yahoo chart-API closes, alarm if |diff| > 0.1% on > 5% of names; (4) drawdown brake — flatten/de-gross when running DD breaches the dev-period p95 block-bootstrap DD envelope (pre-registered).

corpus_priors.name: bounded_ai_benchmark_charisma_vs_live_competence
corpus_priors.content: The corpus's AI verdict is role-specific: supervised ML is mature for regularized cross-sectional ranking; RL is credible mainly in bounded control problems; foundation models are priors, weak in finance off-the-shelf; LLM/agentic layers are bounded copilots, and LiveTradeBench shows a real gap between static-benchmark excellence and live trading outcomes. 'Autonomous LLM trader near production' and 'broad sim-to-live RL transfer' sit explicitly in the LOW-confidence tier, and this late layer carries acknowledged recency/preprint bias — hold it more skeptically than the 1975-2010 lessons.
corpus_priors.horizon: months (paper-track accumulation); architecture-permanent for the authority constraint
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Revised only by reproducible multi-market live evidence of autonomous cores beating modular stacks net of costs; until then any AI component with direct order authority and no sandbox is a listed deprecated approach.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Two parts: (1) architectural invariant — AI/RL outputs may only set an exposure dial in a pre-registered discrete set behind the frozen gate (already the project's substrate optimum: every lever expresses through the dial); (2) measurable benchmark-vs-live gap = rolling 63d realized paper-track Sharpe minus backtest Sharpe on the identical dates from the daily book, drift alarm at |z| > 2. The autonomy question itself is NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY beyond this paper/live divergence proxy.

corpus_priors.name: system_over_model_doctrine
corpus_priors.content: The full 1975-2026 corpus finds NO universal 'most profitable model'. Durable profitability is a property of the whole chain — weak, regime-dependent, fast-decaying signal passed through point-in-time data, search-penalized validation, uncertainty/cost-aware allocation, an independent risk veto, realistic execution, and fail-safe operations. The signal is almost always weaker than it looks in-sample; the production layer matters more than the predictor.
corpus_priors.horizon: years (doctrine level, applies at every decision horizon)
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Breaks as a claim only if a single model class is shown to reproducibly beat modular stacks net of costs across regimes in live trading — the corpus explicitly says no such evidence exists through 2026.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Promotion gate, computable from the daily panel: a candidate reaches capital only if net-of-cost Sharpe on the FROZEN walk-forward holdout beats both (a) equal-weight buy-and-hold of the same universe and (b) the current champion, with the full trial count recorded. This is already the project's frozen-gate practice; the atom pins it as doctrine, not preference.

Panel-only fallback partition: stress = (21d return of the equal-weight Dow-30 close index < 0) AND (cross-sectional std of daily returns > its 63d median). Require the candidate's edge to be sign-stable in both partitions, or ship it with an explicit regime scope condition.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Fails when the regime classifier itself is degenerate (one state absorbing >95% of days) or when the evaluation window contains only one regime — then the test is vacuous and must be deferred, not passed.
corpus_priors.horizon: weeks-to-months (regime dwell times); rule itself permanent
