corpus_priors.name: market_weather_vector
corpus_priors.content: The root risk/cash layer should encode 'market weather' from breadth, stress, and regime information rather than any single price series; the group layer routes capital and the stock layer picks residual opportunity. The economic state engine's core groups are: inflation/real-rate regime, credit expansion/contraction, liquidity and funding stress, volatility/correlation regime, and breadth/concentration.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Daily 4-part weather vector: (1) breadth = fraction of Dow-30 tickers with close > their 50d MA, from the per-ticker panel; (2) cross-sectional dispersion = std of the 30 same-day returns; (3) vol term structure = ^VIX/^VIX3M close ratio (>1 = inverted/stressed); (4) credit stress = latest Fed EBP (monthly, forward-filled, flag staleness) plus 21d log return of the HYG/LQD price ratio from Yahoo.
corpus_priors.horizon: days-weeks (inputs update daily; regime meaning shifts over months)
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Any single component used as a return forecast; breadth is degenerate on only 30 names during narrow-leadership markets; EBP is monthly and revised, so it is stale between releases.

corpus_priors.name: regime_gated_exposure_not_forecast
corpus_priors.content: Regime dependence means the risk layer must be state-dependent, not constant-high-cash, and must be penalized for hiding in cash during constructive regimes. The state engine's output is gates and risk-budget multipliers — it should gate and contextualize strategies, not mechanically forecast returns without validation.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Exposure dial as a monotone map of the existing 2-state P(bear) belief. Cash-shortcut audit, daily-computable: mean invested fraction on 'constructive' days (breadth > 0.6 AND ^VIX below its 252d median) must stay above a preset floor (e.g. 0.7); flag any policy that violates it.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: One-regime overfit; belief lag at regime turns; silently converting the gate into a return predictor.

corpus_priors.name: promise_enforceability_mispricing
corpus_priors.content: The economy is a layered promise system (money, credit, earnings, collateral, policy backstops, liquidity, narratives); tradable opportunity appears when the market misprices the current ENFORCEABILITY of promises under changing constraints (rates, vol, liquidity, crowding, cost). A trade is valid only if the promise/constraint mismatch is observable at decision time and executable after costs. The source itself flags this theory as rejectable in three ways: it can become too broad (explains everything after the fact), it can hide narrative overfit, and a readable hierarchy is not automatically profitable.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: The general theory is not a daily signal; only named instantiations bind: refinancing-promise stress = Fed EBP (ffilled) plus 21d change of the HYG/IEF price ratio; liquidity/vol-promise stress = ^VIX/^VIX3M > 1.0; policy-rate constraint = 63d change in ^TNX. Every new promise-state hypothesis must name its own point-in-time observable before testing, else reject.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Used without a pre-registered observable it degenerates to post-hoc storytelling; macro narrative can mask a weak stock-level signal.

corpus_priors.name: credit_amplification_late_derisking
corpus_priors.content: Credit and leverage amplify shocks (Schularick-Taylor anchor): credit stress weakens fragile balance sheets before earnings reflect it. The CHRL translation is a risk-break trigger plus drawdown control, and the named canonical failure is LATE de-risking.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: De-risking trigger, daily-computable: (Fed EBP latest value above the 75th percentile of its published history) OR (21d log return of HYG/IEF < -3%); response = cap the exposure dial, never raise it. EBP monthly staleness must be flagged as data-age in the state.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: 2022-style rate-driven selloffs move HYG and IEF together, muting the ratio; EBP revisions; trigger fires late in fast crashes — it is a cycle tool, not a crash detector.

corpus_priors.name: executability_before_alpha
corpus_priors.content: Liquidity is endogenous and execution is part of alpha: an edge is real only after fees, slippage, impact, capacity, and turnover. The named failure to avoid is high gross alpha destroyed by costs, so turnover gates and cost stress are mandatory before any promotion.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Net edge = gross edge - (daily turnover x cost-per-change, project spec 30bp/change); daily turnover = 0.5 x sum|delta w| from executed weights. Per-ticker liquidity proxy = 21d mean Amihud illiquidity |return|/(close x volume) from the panel. Acceptance requires net-of-cost outperformance vs EW Dow-30 buy-and-hold AND vs T-bill cash (^IRX accrued).
corpus_priors.horizon: all horizons; binds at every evaluation
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Cost model too optimistic; true capacity and impact are not measurable from daily OHLCV alone — treat capacity claims as unverified.

corpus_priors.name: alpha_half_life_vs_execution_delay
corpus_priors.content: Forecast quality is only valuable when the alpha half-life exceeds execution delay plus cost recovery; open crowding reduces future executable alpha. A signal that dies at a 1-day lag is not tradable in this bot's daily-close pipeline.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Half-life curve computable on the panel: measure the signal's edge (next-h return spread or IC) at execution lags 0, 1, 2, 5 days; require the 1-day-lag edge to still clear the cost hurdle. Crowding itself has no daily OHLCV proxy — only the half-life measurement binds.
corpus_priors.horizon: days (the lag test); signal lifetimes of months-years
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Half-life estimates are noisy on short samples; a signal can pass the lag test in one regime and fail in another.

corpus_priors.name: feature_maximalism_null
corpus_priors.content: The RL-Based Feature Ablation branch closed as a negative result: adding more feature families, stress reward reweighting, action penalties, and latent/action interventions did NOT reliably improve OOS trading. The bottleneck was likely model structure/stability, not feature quantity; keep compact macro context instead of feature soup, and treat the failed branch as a constraint on future search.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Binds to the evaluation protocol, computable on the panel: any candidate feature family must beat an obs-dimension-matched noise-placebo twin on the frozen OOS window; reject otherwise. No daily market series carries this atom — it prunes the hypothesis space.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Over-generalizing: the null is local to this stack and daily data; a genuinely new information source (text, intraday) is outside its scope.

corpus_priors.name: generic_sentiment_null
corpus_priors.content: Raw/flat text features HURT the older flat PPO; only compact, semantically aligned text groups helped CHRL, modestly, and the best group was forward-looking/earnings (earnings_pressure, numeric_evidence_density, signal_confidence). Macro text belongs only in risk/regime gates, never as direct stock alpha; results remain fold/seed-limited and some text variants worsen drawdown.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY — requires the point-in-time text corpus, provenance fields, and LLM extraction pipeline; nothing in the OHLCV panels or free public series carries these features. Binds only as a routing rule if/when text re-enters: company text to stock layer, macro text to the risk gate, quality scores to reliability gates.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Treating the modest CHRL text lift as established alpha; it never passed multi-fold, multi-seed validation.

corpus_priors.name: search_aware_validation_firewall
corpus_priors.content: Every experiment consumes a trial budget and the best backtest among many trials is suspicious by default (White reality check, PBO). Because the self-evolving loop multiplies tested ideas, the validation firewall must get STRONGER as the loop gets faster. Reject gains concentrated in one fold, one regime, one ticker, or one lucky event, and reject improvements explained only by higher risk or lower cash.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Computable battery on the panel: (a) mandatory baselines = EW Dow-30 buy-and-hold, no-trade, and ^IRX-accrued cash; (b) placebo distribution from >=100 date-shuffled/sign-flipped runs of the same rule, require candidate z above the placebo spread; (c) trial-count ledger feeding a deflated-Sharpe correction; (d) frozen holdout touched once.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Firewall weaker than the proposal rate; evaluator itself edited during a strategy loop (explicitly forbidden); placebos not exposure-matched (the project's own z_dsd-degeneracy lesson).

corpus_priors.name: independent_risk_shell
corpus_priors.content: Risk authority must live OUTSIDE the alpha engine: hard deterministic limits (max order, position, gross exposure, daily loss, drawdown, turnover, stale-data tolerance, live/backtest divergence) with graded actions (throttle, reduce, block, safe-flat, rollback). No LLM/RL module gets autonomous capital access, self-modifying trading code, or unlogged model changes without these guardrails (SEC 15c3-5 anchor).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: All daily-computable from the executed book plus the panel: running drawdown of book equity; daily P&L; daily turnover = 0.5 x sum|delta w|; stale-data = calendar days since the last panel row (block if above tolerance); live/backtest divergence = tracking difference between the paper book and a frozen-replay book on identical dates.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Shell parameters tunable by the self-improvement loop (must be constitution-frozen); divergence check meaningless before a paper track exists.

corpus_priors.name: residual_selection_not_stories
corpus_priors.content: Technology/narrative truth is not investment truth: new cash-flow possibility does not automatically become shareholder capture (example: AI capex pays infrastructure suppliers before adopters capture productivity). The stock layer must therefore encode RESIDUAL opportunity — separating general market movement from idiosyncratic potential — and must not buy merely because the whole market bounced.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Per-ticker residual return = r_i minus EW Dow-30 return, daily; 21d residual momentum and residual vol per ticker from the panel; audit = stock-layer signal must have low correlation with the market factor. The AI-capex-specific claim is NOT measurable from the Dow-30 OHLCV panel (no capex/fundamental data).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: This bot is empirically a cash-timer with a near-equal-weight book, so selection value is unproven here; residuals on 30 names are noisy and sector-confounded.

corpus_priors.name: backstop_calm_not_permanent
corpus_priors.content: Institutions and policy backstops reshape the risk distribution and can manufacture calm regimes that are rescue-driven rather than structural; the named failure is mistaking rescue for permanent calm. Era anchors (Great Inflation, BIS 'Is Price Stability Enough', credit-booms-gone-bust): long price-stable calm can incubate leverage that later amplifies the bust.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Calm-with-stress divergence flag, daily-computable: (^VIX below its 252d 25th percentile) AND (Fed EBP above its historical 75th percentile OR curve proxy ^TNX minus ^IRX < 0). Emit as a scenario WARNING that tightens risk budget — never as a return forecast.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The flag can stay on for years without an event (inversion 2022-23); using it to time entries/exits rather than to size risk budgets.

corpus_priors.name: promotion_discipline_creed
corpus_priors.content: The previous compression's best distillation, worth keeping verbatim: 'Regime before rule. Timing before signal. Costs before alpha. Liquidity before confidence. Failure conditions before promotion.' Every candidate rule is evaluated in that order, and a rule failing an earlier step never reaches a later one.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Enforce as computable gate checks on any backtest from the daily panels: (1) per-regime performance split using the bot's 2-state belief P(bear); (2) signal-lag audit (signal computed at close t, executed t+1 only); (3) net return after turnover x 30bp per change; (4) performance conditioned on a liquidity state (21d Amihud z of traded names); (5) a written failure-condition list filed before promotion.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Becomes ritual if the checks run but never veto. A rule re-tuned on the same window after failing costs is a NEW rule requiring fresh out-of-window validation.
corpus_priors.horizon: evaluation-time, applies at all trading horizons

corpus_priors.name: calm_leverage_fragility_loop
corpus_priors.content: The corpus's master cycle: stable regime -> lower perceived risk -> spread compression / leverage / crowding -> shock -> liquidity, funding, and narrative break -> forced adjustment -> new backstop -> risk migrates elsewhere. Long calm is a fragility accumulator, not a safety certificate.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Calm-spell clock: consecutive days with (a) 21d realized vol of the equal-weight Dow-30 book below its trailing 756d median AND (b) VIX close below its trailing 756d 25th percentile. Use only as a confidence-downgrade input on long-exposure/carry rules, never as a standalone short signal.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Calm spells extend for years (2013-14, 2017); trading the clock as timing loses to carry. Duration/age clocks have repeatedly failed as triggers — the mechanism is real, its timing is not predictable.

corpus_priors.name: stress_exit_correlation_spike
corpus_priors.content: Liquidity is endogenous: a market looks liquid until many participants need the same exit at the same time. In that state forced selling and funding mechanics dominate fundamentals — cross-sectional structure collapses into one market factor and name-level information temporarily stops pricing.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Two proxies from the per-ticker panel: (1) 21d mean pairwise correlation of Dow-30 daily returns (equivalently the variance ratio var(EW book return)/mean var(name returns)); (2) same-day down-breadth = fraction of Dow-30 names with return < -1.5 x their own 63d sigma. Spikes mark the forced-flow state: gate OFF stock-selection rules and hand control to the cash/exposure dial.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Mostly contemporaneous, not leading — by the time correlation spikes the first leg has happened. State descriptor and risk gate only, never an entry signal.

corpus_priors.name: credit_is_access_not_price
corpus_priors.content: Credit amplification: credit is not only a spread level but access, collateral, maturity, refinancing need, and trust. Credit stress tends to lead equity stress at monthly horizons because refinancing walls bind before earnings do; deteriorating credit under calm equity vol is a divergence warning.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Daily proxy: 21d change of the HYG/IEF close ratio (Yahoo ETFs) as the credit-spread stand-in; use HYG/LQD to cancel part of the duration component. Slow proxy: the Fed's monthly EBP series with >=1-month publication lag (already present in the bot's panel).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: HYG/IEF mixes duration with credit — a pure rate shock (2022-style) moves it without credit stress. EBP is monthly and revised; naive daily credit gates on equities have failed kill tests — treat as state input, not signal.

corpus_priors.name: inflation_rate_regime_resets_meaning
corpus_priors.content: 1975-1992 lesson: inflation and disinflation change the meaning of valuation, nominal profits, leverage, and historical averages — coefficients estimated in one inflation/rate regime mislead in another. Money is a credibility contract; while credibility is being re-priced, real rates and refinancing dominate price formation.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Rate-shock flag from free daily series: 252d change of the 10y yield (^TNX) > +150bp, OR 63d realized vol of daily yield changes in its top trailing-3y decile. When flagged, discount any rule whose calibration window contains no flagged period, and report per-flag performance separately.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The bot's usable daily history contains essentially ONE inflation-shock episode (2021-2023), so per-flag statistics are underpowered — never fit thresholds to that single episode.

corpus_priors.name: tech_truth_is_not_investment_truth
corpus_priors.content: 1993-2010 and the AI era: a real innovation can be overcapitalized, overfinanced, or captured by suppliers, customers, or labor rather than equity holders — adoption, productivity, monetization, and shareholder capture are separate steps. Growth, volume, and recent success are not enough; ask who captures the surplus and who funds the risk.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Mostly NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY (narrative/fundamental layer). Weak crowding descriptor only: z-score of the 252d relative performance of the Dow-30 tech subset (AAPL, MSFT, CSCO, IBM, INTC, CRM) vs the equal-weight remainder — a crowding state marker, explicitly NOT a short signal (proxy itself is Speculative).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Relative-strength stretch persisted for years (2015-2021, 2023-2024 megacap runs); trimming or shorting on stretch alone lost repeatedly.

corpus_priors.name: forbidden_inference_list
corpus_priors.content: The compression's second-best block: the bot must never infer that low volatility means low risk, low CPI means financial stability, risk transfer means risk elimination, a factor backtest is a law, a behavioral bias is automatically tradeable alpha, gross return suffices without net-of-cost, a narrative is alpha without a market-structure carrier, or that one instrument/regime pocket justifies promotion.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Three items bind as computable vetoes: (1) the risk state must combine vol (VIX or realized) with credit (HYG/IEF 21d change) and breadth (fraction of names above their 50d MA) — never vol alone; (2) the bar is net return at turnover x 30bp, never gross; (3) promotion requires breadth: positive contribution in a majority of Dow-30 names or in both regime states, computed from the per-ticker panel.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The list prunes hypotheses; it does not generate them. Passing all vetoes is necessary, not sufficient.

corpus_priors.name: corpus_provenance_holes
corpus_priors.content: The inventory proves holes the previous compression only half-declared: there is NO 1-year report for 2009 or 2022, the 2008 file is a 2,702-word stub (about one-third normal length), 1993-1996 and 2012-2013 have duplicate/alternative reports, and 2026-2027 material is scenario, not realized history. Any corpus-derived memory of 2008-09 or 2022 — the two most instructive crash episodes for the bot's dev windows — comes only from the coarser 3/9/18-year summary layers.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY — a provenance rule for the memory store: tag corpus-derived semantic memories dated 2008, 2009, 2022, 2026, 2027 as low-provenance or scenario, and never let them outweigh the bot's own panel data, which DOES cover 2022 with real prices.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Untagged, the corpus's weakest years get equal weight with its strongest, and scenario text about 2027 can leak into the world model as fact.

corpus_priors.name: hypothesis_bank_mistargeted
corpus_priors.content: The previous compression's entire actionable half (HYP-EWP-001..010) is written for Chinese commodity futures — open interest, roll boundaries, day/night sessions, next-open execution. The current bot's world model (daily OHLCV equities) cannot measure open interest, rolls, or sessions at all, so six of ten hypotheses are unbindable as written; this instrument mistargeting is the core reason the compression was judged insufficient.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Three hypotheses survive translation and bind: HYP-001 regime-gated trend -> gate trend rules on P(bear) plus breadth (fraction of names above 50d MA); HYP-004 family breadth -> require name-level trend to agree with sector-bucket breadth (Dow-30 sectors / csi500 industry groups from the per-ticker panel); HYP-008 cost-first sparsification -> hysteresis bands + minimum holding period, scored as turnover reduction at matched gross signal. HYP-003/006/007 (open interest, sessions, rolls) = NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY on this data.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: If commodity-futures data ever enters the world model, the six parked hypotheses re-open — park them in memory, do not delete them.

corpus_priors.name: regime_dependence_gate
corpus_priors.content: Regime dependence is the corpus's #1 durable mechanism: old coefficients break when inflation, rates, policy reaction functions, liquidity, or market structure change, so no rule earns trust from one macro regime. The operational corollary is to condition rules on an observable regime state rather than hunting for regime-free rules.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Bind regime state as a small daily vector: [P(bear) from the 2-state belief; fraction of Dow-30 names above their 50d MA; 21d realized-vol percentile of the EW book vs trailing 756d; VIX/VIX3M close ratio]. Every rule's scorecard must show performance per quartile of this vector.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The corpus's own stated failure mode for HYP-001: a regime gate can look good purely by trading less and avoiding costs — always compare against a no-trade baseline and an exposure-matched unconditioned twin.

corpus_priors.name: rule_coefficient_drift_monitor
corpus_priors.content: Because backstops re-route risk ('risk migrates elsewhere' closes the corpus's master loop), every calibrated rule carries an implicit expiry, and failed rules must be preserved because negative knowledge narrows the future hypothesis space. The previous compression stated this but attached no clock — this atom adds one.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Drift monitor from the panels: for each live rule, rolling 252d sign-consistency and IC of its signal vs realized forward returns, compared with calibration-window values; alarm when the sign flips or |IC| halves for two consecutive quarters. Killed rules go into the semantic memory store with their failure signature, never deleted.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: A 252d-window alarm fires 1-2 years after decay begins — it bounds losses, it does not prevent them. An alarm during a single regime spike is not decay; check the regime vector first.

corpus_priors.name: daily_liquidity_stress_gauge
corpus_priors.content: Behavior matters only when structural: narrative and herding become investable only when carried by leverage, forced selling, margin, or mandate constraints. The daily-visible shadow of those carriers is abnormal volume attached to abnormal down-moves, and constrained-arbitrage pockets (where mean-reversion can work) exist mainly in that state.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: From per-ticker OHLCV: (1) Amihud illiquidity |ret|/(close x volume), 21d mean, z-scored per name vs trailing 252d, aggregated as the panel median; (2) forced-flow day flag: EW book return < -1.5 x its 63d sigma AND panel-median volume > 1.5 x its 63d median. Elevated readings license mean-reversion rules; normal readings do not.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Volume spikes also come from index rebalances, earnings, and option expiries with zero stress content; csi500 volume semantics (retail share, price limits) differ from Dow-30 — calibrate per panel, never pool.

corpus_priors.name: calm_breeds_fragility
corpus_priors.content: The single most recurrent claim across the 1985/2005/2006/2007/2017/2018 reports: sustained calm with compressed risk premia is when fragility accumulates ('stability -> leverage drift -> crisis'; 'the most dangerous assets have smooth returns'; 'treat low volatility as a possible warning sign'). Low vol and tight spreads are not an all-clear — they shift the return distribution toward a fatter left tail while carrying no timing information.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: calm-spell counter = consecutive trading days with ^VIX close below its trailing 252d median, computed daily; secondary confirmation = HYG/IEF 63d total-return ratio within 1% of its 252d high. Spells > 60d should raise the bot's tail-risk prior (e.g., widen the drawdown budget used in position sizing), never increase exposure.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Calm spells can run for years (2004-06, 2012-14, 2016-17); using this as a short/de-risk timing signal loses carry badly. It is a distribution-shape prior, not a trigger.

corpus_priors.name: intermediary_deleveraging_spiral
corpus_priors.content: Adrian-Shin / Brunnermeier line (2007, 2010, 2020, 2025 reports): intermediary balance sheets are procyclical — price falls raise haircuts/margins, forcing sales that push prices down further; in these episodes the equity discount rate moves because of plumbing, not earnings revisions. The 2025 report stresses this now runs through NBFI/private credit/repo, so small shocks can produce disproportionately large moves, hitting long-duration equities and leveraged cyclicals hardest.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: plumbing-stress flag = (SPY 5d return < -3%) AND (IEF 5d return <= 0, i.e., Treasuries NOT rallying) AND (fraction of Dow-30 names down on the day > 0.9). The joint stock+Treasury selloff with near-total downside breadth is the daily-observable fingerprint of forced deleveraging (March 2020 pattern) vs ordinary risk-off (where IEF rallies).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Rate-shock selloffs (2022) also produce joint stock+bond declines without a funding spiral — require a concurrent VIX jump (VIX 5d change > +8 pts) to separate the two. ETF proxies only exist post-2007.

corpus_priors.name: policy_backstop_reflex
corpus_priors.content: The 2020 report's core mechanism: forced-selling dislocations plus a credible policy backstop -> tail risk truncated -> asset prices recover far faster than the real economy ('policy backstop -> lower tail risk -> faster asset-price recovery than real-economy recovery'). The dislocation low is a liquidity/flow event, not a rational solvency repricing, so it mean-reverts once the backstop lands.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: backstop-recovery state = ^VIX closed above 40 within the last 60 trading days AND has since closed back below 30, while the Dow-30 equal-weight index is still > 15% below its 252d high. The VIX retreat is the observable trace of an effective backstop (the bot cannot see policy directly). Enter/extend exposure in this state rather than waiting for price confirmation.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: n is small (2009, 2020). Fails in grind-down bears with no acute spike (2000-02) and when inflation constrains the backstop (2022 — VIX never sustained >40, no reflex fired). Do not fire on VIX spikes without the >15%-drawdown condition.

corpus_priors.name: stock_bond_correlation_regime
corpus_priors.content: The 2023 report makes stock-bond correlation an explicit regime variable: 'inflation uncertainty -> unstable stock-bond diversification', 'assume diversification is regime-dependent', and it names the return of persistently negative correlation as a fact that would weaken its own narrative. Inflation-dominant regimes flip the correlation positive and kill the bond hedge — exactly what the project's own P-1 kill found for 2022.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: rolling 63d Pearson correlation of SPY vs IEF daily close-to-close returns (both free via Yahoo), computed daily. corr > 0 = inflation-dominant regime (bonds do not hedge; cash is the only hedge asset); corr < -0.2 = growth-shock regime (Treasury rallies confirm equity risk-off). Feed as a third input alongside the existing 2-state belief.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: 63d estimate is noisy and lags regime turns by ~1-2 months; mid-range values (-0.2 to 0) are uninformative.

corpus_priors.name: credit_conditions_primary_input
corpus_priors.content: 'Treat credit conditions as a primary valuation input' (2014) and 'compressed spreads are warning signals / separate credit growth from credit quality' (2007) recur across nearly every year: credit is the cycle amplifier (Schularick-Taylor, Jorda et al.), and spread dynamics lead equity trouble. The 2024 report adds that credit migration to opaque private markets delays repricing, making the observable spread series move late but violently.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: two-speed construction: (a) slow = Fed EBP level and its 3m change (monthly, forward-filled daily — already a PK-survivor in the project's panel v2); (b) fast = 21d total-return spread HYG minus IEF (Yahoo, daily). Deteriorating-credit flag = HYG-IEF 21d spread in its bottom trailing-3y quintile OR EBP 3m change > +0.3 while equities are flat/up over the same 21d (the divergence, not the level, is the signal).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: HYG-IEF mixes duration and flow effects and exists only post-2007; EBP is monthly and revised. Spread widening that merely accompanies an equity selloff adds no information — only the lead/divergence does.

corpus_priors.name: extrapolation_momentum_overshoot
corpus_priors.content: Extrapolative/diagnostic expectations plus limited attention (2014 p514, 2017 Bordalo-Gennaioli-Shleifer, 2018) make momentum and bubble-overshoot coexist: gradual news is under-incorporated (drift) while spectacular narratives overshoot and reverse. The named indicators are price acceleration without comparable cash-flow improvement, flows, turnover, and media intensity; the mechanism breaks when hard information arrives fast or arbitrage capital is abundant.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: per-ticker from the OHLCV panel: acceleration = (21d return) minus (prior 21d return); crowding proxy = z-score of 21d average dollar volume vs trailing 252d. Overshoot-risk (reversal-prone) = acceleration in top decile AND volume z > 2; steady-trend (drift-prone) = positive 21d return with acceleration near 0 and normal volume. Media/flows/fundamentals legs are NOT in the panel — volume z is the only crowding proxy available.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The project's own broad-universe tests found plain momentum dead and reversal weak-net-of-cost on recent US daily data — so this atom is only actionable in its conditional form (acceleration x volume), and must be validated against exposure-matched twins before deployment, per the project's trim-rule lesson.
corpus_priors.horizon: weeks to months for drift; days to weeks for post-overshoot reversal

corpus_priors.name: no_monocausal_gating
corpus_priors.content: Recurring negative knowledge (2012: 'models without financial frictions are dangerous'; 2015/2016: 'avoid monocausal narratives'; 2005: use multiple models as 'orthogonal checks' that argue with each other). No single indicator family — vol, credit, or breadth — is a reliable regime gate on its own; each has documented false-positive modes.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: risk-off regime transitions require >= 2 of 3 independent families to confirm within a 5-day window: (a) vol: ^VIX above its trailing 252d 80th percentile OR ^VIX/^VIX3M > 1.0; (b) credit: HYG-IEF 21d spread in bottom trailing-3y quintile OR EBP 3m rise; (c) breadth: fraction of Dow-30 names above their own 50d MA < 0.40. All three computable daily from the existing panel + free series.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The 2-of-3 rule trades speed for reliability — it will lag single-day crashes (Feb 2018) by 1-3 days; accept that cost rather than loosening to 1-of-3.

corpus_priors.name: breadth_over_index_level
corpus_priors.content: The pre-2027 report's principle 'monitor breadth, not only index level' plus the 2019-2026 superstar-concentration mechanism ('intangible scale + concentration -> margin dispersion'): the index can make highs on a few names while the median stock deteriorates, which is a fragility signature, not strength. Concentration means index-level series increasingly mismeasure the market's internal state.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: daily from the per-ticker Dow-30 panel: breadth = fraction of names above their own 50d MA; divergence flag = equal-weight Dow-30 makes a 252d closing high while breadth < 0.6, OR (equal-weight 21d return) minus (best-performing-quintile-weighted 21d return) < -2%. Directly composable with the project's existing new-high-arming lead.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Dow-30 is only 30 names — breadth is coarse (steps of 1/30) and the Dow lacks the mega-cap concentration the mechanism describes; the csi500 panel is the better substrate for this atom. Formal predictive evidence for breadth divergence is mixed.

corpus_priors.name: rate_equity_shock_quadrant
corpus_priors.content: 'Low rates are not low risk' (2021 p602, 2016, 2005 saving-glut) and 'r<g does not make debt free' (2019 p514): the LEVEL of rates carries no risk signal because low rates can reflect glut, overhang, or demographics while fragility rises. What is informative daily is the joint SIGN of rate and equity moves, which decomposes shocks: yields up + stocks down = inflation/rate shock (bonds won't hedge); yields down + stocks down = growth/flight shock (bonds hedge, credit is the risk).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: quadrant = (sign of 21d change in ^TNX, sign of 21d SPY return), computed daily from free Yahoo series. Route the regime response on the quadrant: (up, down) -> reduce duration-sensitive exposure, treat IEF as non-hedging; (down, down) -> classic risk-off, credit flags dominate. Never use the ^TNX level itself as a risk gauge.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: 21d windows mix overlapping shocks; quadrant near zero on either axis is noise — require |Δ^TNX 21d| > 20bp and |SPY 21d| > 2% before classifying.

corpus_priors.name: valuation_has_no_daily_timing_power
corpus_priors.content: The 2000 report's principle — 'use valuation for horizon and margin-of-safety, not for precise timing' — is stated as the correct reading of the dot-com peak: valuation-scale signals (CAPE, DY) predict multi-year returns but are useless for daily/weekly decisions, and shorting on valuation alone dies before it pays (limits to arbitrage, 1994/1995 reports). The project's own W3 CAPE falsification independently confirms.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY — the OHLCV panel carries no earnings/dividend fundamentals, and the honest lesson is that even if imported (e.g., frozen Shiller data), valuation must NOT be bound to any daily action; it belongs only in multi-year capital-allocation layers (the personalization track), never in the daily bot's decision loop.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: n/a — this atom's job is to block a class of bindings, not to fire.
corpus_priors.horizon: years (hence out of the daily bot's scope)

corpus_priors.name: regimes_not_average_coefficients
corpus_priors.content: The 1977 Lucas-critique lesson, restated as investor principle #1 ('analyze regimes, not average coefficients') and echoed by the 1975-77 synthesis: macro-financial relationships are not timeless constants — they depend on expectations, policy rules, contracts, and financial structure, so any coefficient estimated across regimes will break out-of-regime. This is the deepest justification for the bot's regime-belief architecture and for refusing pooled-sample signal estimates.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: a procedure, fully computable: estimate and evaluate every candidate signal separately within each state of the bot's existing 2-state regime belief (and within the stock-bond-correlation regime above); a signal whose sign flips between regime states is regime-dependent information, not noise — pooled estimation would average it to zero. Ship only regime-conditioned rules.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Conditioning halves the effective sample per state — on short histories this trades bias for variance; require each within-regime estimate to clear its own significance bar, not the pooled one.

corpus_priors.name: era_contingency_of_learned_rules
corpus_priors.content: The 2021 report's self-critique (p644) is a general decay law: lessons learned inside an exceptional era (pandemic plumbing, ZIRP) may be contingent on that era, not durable — 'part of the synthesis would have to be weakened' if the old structure returns. Rules trained wholly inside one rate/inflation era (e.g., 2010-2021 ZIRP, where bonds hedged and dips mean-reverted fast) carry an expiry that triggers when the era variable flips, as 2022 demonstrated.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: tag every learned rule with its training-era bucket using ^IRX (3m T-bill, free Yahoo, already used in the project's E-23 eye set): ZIRP era = ^IRX < 0.5%, positive-rate era = ^IRX > 2.0%, transition otherwise. Decay flag fires when the live bucket differs from the training bucket for > 63 consecutive trading days -> quarantine the rule (shadow-book it) until re-certified in the new era through the frozen gate.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: ^IRX is a crude era proxy (misses inflation-vs-real decomposition); bucket thresholds are heuristic; a rule can also decay within an era (crowding), which this clock does not detect.

corpus_priors.name: regime_before_backtest
corpus_priors.content: The synthesis's core epistemic law: policy-regime shifts change agent behavior and therefore break historical coefficients (master mechanism: policy_regime_shift -> adaptive_behavior -> broken_backtests; regime dependence tagged High confidence). Any rule validated inside one regime silently assumes that regime's continuation, so regime awareness must precede any backtest read.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Label every training/eval day with a daily-computable regime tuple: (a) sign of the 63d rolling correlation between Dow-30 equal-weight daily return and IEF daily return (Yahoo), (b) 10y real yield above/below zero (FRED DFII10, daily), (c) the bot's own 2-state P(bear). Candidate rules must be evaluated within each regime cell and either survive across cells or ship explicitly regime-gated.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Regime labels are themselves estimates (63d correlation is noisy — use +/-0.2 hysteresis); slicing by too many regime axes shrinks samples and invites multiplicity, so cap the tuple at these three axes.
corpus_priors.horizon: regimes last years; enforcement is daily
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: stock_bond_correlation_regime_1
corpus_priors.content: The largest 52-year reversal is inflation-dominant vs demand-shock regimes, and its market-revealed signature is the sign of stock-bond correlation: positive under inflation constraint (1970s-early 90s, 2022 shock) so duration does not hedge equities and cash is the hedge; negative under disinflationary demand shocks (late 1990s-2010s) so duration hedges. The project independently rediscovered this in P-1 (2022 broke the stock-bond hedge).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: 63d rolling correlation of Dow-30 EW daily return vs IEF daily return (Yahoo). Flag inflation-constrained when corr > +0.2, demand-shock regime when corr < -0.2, hysteresis between. In the inflation-constrained state, any hedge/diversifier sleeve must be cash/T-bills (^IRX-accruing, as the executor already does), never duration.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Slow variable — roughly 1-2 sign flips per decade, and the 63d estimator lags true turns by 1-2 months. US-scope only; does not transfer to csi500 (project's E-09 precedent: live US macro does not lift csi500).
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: backstop_credibility_state
corpus_priors.content: The crisis -> backstop -> moral-hazard cycle means dip-buying pays only while policy is free to rescue; the pandemic aftermath shows backstops run into inflation, fiscal, and legitimacy constraints. When inflation expectations are rising the central bank cannot backstop drawdowns and stress does not mean-revert (2022); when expectations are anchored, spikes revert fast (2010s, March 2020).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Two daily proxies: (a) VIX/VIX3M ratio — ratio > 1.0 marks a stress-test state, and days-until-renormalization is the backstop-credibility clock (renormalize within ~10 trading days = backstopped dip; persist > ~20 days = backstop credibility itself in question); (b) 63d change in the 10y breakeven (FRED T10YIE, daily) — positive breakeven drift concurrent with rising 10y yields (^TNX) = policy-constrained state: suppress buy-the-dip responses.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Small sample of true backstop tests; the term-structure clock also inverts on non-policy shocks (single events, elections); breakevens embed liquidity premia in stress (March 2020 breakevens collapsed for liquidity reasons, not expectations).
corpus_priors.horizon: days-weeks for the VIX clock; months for the breakeven drift
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: credit_price_vs_credit_quantity
corpus_priors.content: Credit is causal, not background (credit_boom -> collateral_feedback -> leverage -> fragility), and funding quality deteriorates before earnings — read liabilities before earnings near turns. But the validated long-horizon crisis predictor (Schularick-Taylor) is credit QUANTITY (credit/GDP growth, quarterly, lagged), while what is observable daily is only the PRICE of credit risk (spreads) — a faster but more coincident object; the two must not be conflated.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Daily credit-stress dial = z-score (vs trailing 1y) of the 21d return spread HYG minus IEF (Yahoo), combined with the latest Fed EBP print (monthly, ~1-month lag; already PK-passed in this project). Use as a monotone exposure de-scaler. The Schularick-Taylor credit/GDP gap itself: NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY (BIS quarterly, heavily revised) — do not fake the quantity channel with spread proxies.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Spreads can stay compressed right up to the break (2007); HYG-IEF nets duration but not equity beta, so it partially re-encodes the equity signal; EBP staleness up to ~6 weeks.
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: calm_accumulates_fragility
corpus_priors.content: The recurring cycle is stability -> spread compression -> leverage -> weak underwriting -> fragility: perceived stability lowers risk premia and invites leverage, making the system path-dependent, so low volatility is evidence of risk accumulation as much as of safety (White 2006 and BoE 2007 warned pre-crisis). Negative-knowledge form: low volatility is not low risk, and low CPI is not financial stability.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Calm-spell counter: consecutive trading days with VIX below its trailing 2y median AND 21d realized vol of Dow-30 EW returns below its own 2y median. Bind as a CONSTRAINT, not a signal: audit that no rule makes exposure an increasing function of spell length, and do not relax the drawdown-budget lambda during long spells (> ~120 days).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Explicitly not a timer: calm regimes ran 2+ years (2012-14, 2016-17) and de-risking early forfeits carry. The mechanism says fragility grows, never when it breaks.
corpus_priors.horizon: accumulation over months-years; the break is days
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: macro_dial_not_clock
corpus_priors.content: Operational core of the doctrine: use macro to adjust margin of safety, not to forecast turns — cycles are balance-sheet vulnerability maps, not clocks, and precise crisis timing carries Low confidence in the synthesis's own map. This converges exactly with the project's twice-certified internal result (E-15/E-16/E-28: the conditional exposure dial beats timing; cold PPO re-derives constant dials).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Already bound and validated: exposure as a monotone function of P(bear) with H=10d holding (certified rule P(bear)>0.66 -> 0.74). Enforce as a rule-family constraint: macro/stress inputs may only enter as continuous exposure scalers with holding >= 10d; reject proposals that use macro state for binary daily entry/exit.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: A genuinely timing-rich substrate (intraday, event windows) could overturn the constraint — matches the project's registered lever-T wake condition.
corpus_priors.horizon: enforcement daily; the underlying claims operate at weeks-quarters
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: forced_selling_liquidation_state
corpus_priors.content: Liquidity is a social condition, not an asset property: liquidity_evaporation -> forced_selling -> price/value/liquidity separation. In liquidation states prices are set by whoever must sell, cross-sectional correlations converge toward one, and price temporarily stops carrying fundamental information (March 2020 dash-for-cash is the canonical instance); ask who is the forced seller and who is the natural buyer.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Daily composite from the per-ticker panel: (a) mean pairwise 21d correlation of Dow-30 daily returns (spike above ~0.6 = liquidation state); (b) cross-sectional mean Amihud illiquidity |ret|/(close*volume), z-scored vs trailing 1y; (c) mean (high-low)/close range z-score. When elevated: treat price moves as flow not information — widen the regime-belief's observation noise, de-weight cross-sectional selection signals; expect weeks-scale reversion but do not pre-position for it.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Correlation also spikes on genuine common shocks (macro news) without forced selling; Amihud is noisy for mega-caps; csi500 needs separate baselines (different microstructure, limit-down days). The reversion implication is contextual, not guaranteed.
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: breadth_concentration_state
corpus_priors.content: Prices are set by marginal buyers and sellers, not representative agents; narrative-concentration episodes (dot-com, current AI megacap capex boom) show cap-weight indices rising while breadth narrows, which is why the market-structure dashboard tracks breadth, cap-vs-equal-weight, and concentration. In such states index-level features stop describing the median stock.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: (a) Fraction of Dow-30 names above their own 50d and 200d MA, from the per-ticker panel (same construction on csi500); (b) 21d return of SPY minus RSP (Yahoo) as the cap-minus-equal-weight spread. Use as an additional regime-belief INPUT (breadth-based bear evidence) and as a switch to per-ticker rather than index-level features when breadth < ~40% while the index 21d return is positive.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: As a predictor breadth divergence is historically weak — 2023-25 concentration persisted for years without a break, and the source's own adversarial review allows concentration to be durable productivity rather than fragility. Thresholds are ad hoc: calibrate on the panel, do not import literature values.
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: rate_shock_transmission
corpus_priors.content: Rates act through refinancing access, deposit behavior, duration values, and credit spreads — not just discount rates; the key question is which assets and business models implicitly require one rate regime to continue. The 2020s instance of the chain low_rates -> duration_and_search_for_yield -> rate_shock_fragility: 2022 equity duration compression, then 2023 bank duration/uninsured-deposit runs.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Per-ticker 63d regression beta of daily returns on daily change in the 10y yield (^TNX/10, Yahoo), computed from the Dow-30 panel; book-level yield-beta = exposure-weighted mean. When trailing 63d change in the 10y exceeds +50bp, penalize concentration in negative-yield-beta names and require confirmation from the credit dial (credit_price_vs_credit_quantity atom).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Equity duration betas are regime-unstable (growth names were rate-insensitive pre-2021, hyper-sensitive in 2022) — the beta must stay rolling, never a fixed loading; the 30-name cross-section limits the penalty's statistical power.
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: risk_migration_decay
corpus_priors.content: After every visible repair, risk re-emerges in a newly accepted balance-sheet carrier: inflation debt stress -> bank capital -> securitized housing/repo -> low-rate duration -> private credit/NBFI/AI-infrastructure SPV finance (FSB sizes private credit at roughly $1.5-2T, untested at scale in a severe downturn). Consequence: detectors trained on the last crisis's carrier decay, and the current frontier carriers are mostly invisible in public daily data.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Carrier identification: NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY (private-credit marks, SPV lease obligations, sovereign auction internals are outside the bot's data). Bindable residue: (a) vintage-decay prior — down-weight crisis features validated only on pre-2010 bank-centric episodes; (b) the frontier's first visible footprint in the bot's set would be the daily credit dial widening PLUS a 21d realized-vol spike in IEF (Treasury-plumbing stress) with no equity-earnings catalyst — log that conjunction to episodic memory as a new-carrier-candidate event rather than trading it.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The source's own adversarial review: risk-migration framing can become too elastic (unfalsifiable if any stress anywhere confirms it), and regulation sometimes genuinely reduces total risk rather than relocating it. The current-frontier identification (AI-infra/private credit) is Speculative.
corpus_priors.horizon: migration over years; the detection conjunction is daily
corpus_priors.source: experience/Economic World Picture/_analysis/52y_synthesis_1975_2027.txt (52-year full economic synthesis 1975-2027, fina

corpus_priors.name: two_phase_financial_cycle
corpus_priors.content: At every synthesis scale (3/9/18/52-year) the same structure recurs: long calm phases in which credit, leverage and risk appetite build slowly under low volatility and tight spreads, then short stress phases in which the collateral -> refinancing -> funding-confidence chain breaks and forced deleveraging reprices everything at once. The build is slow, the unwind is fast; the asymmetry is structural. This is the strongest source-level support for a 2-state regime belief as the bot's first-order world variable.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: The existing 2-state belief, with eyes spanning vol + credit + slope + breadth: daily 2-state HMM (or logistic belief) over [21d realized vol of the EW Dow-30 return (per-ticker panel), VIX/VIX3M ratio (Yahoo ^VIX, ^VIX3M), HYG/LQD 21d relative total return (Yahoo), fraction of Dow-30 names above their 50d MA (per-ticker panel), 10y-3m slope (^TNX, ^IRX)]. All daily-computable.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Two states compress real heterogeneity: inflationary stress (1981, 2022) and deflationary/credit stress (2008, 2020) are different bears; a belief trained only on deflationary bears mis-reads a rate-shock bear. Regime phase lengths (months-years) far exceed the daily update cadence — do not confuse belief noise with phase change.
corpus_priors.horizon: phases last months-years; belief updates daily; stress phases weeks-months

corpus_priors.name: vol_term_backwardation_stress_marker
corpus_priors.content: The fast-unwind episodes the source dwells on (1987, 1998, 2008, 2020) are liquidity events, not information events: funding stress, forced sellers, dealer constraints. Their cleanest same-day fingerprint inside the bot's free input set is inversion of the volatility term structure — spot fear priced above forward fear.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: VIX/VIX3M ratio from Yahoo (^VIX, ^VIX3M); stress flag = ratio > 1.0, intensity = ratio level. Corroborated project-side: backwardation was the sole survivor of the E-24 kill battery (p=0.0003) and the graduated v12 rule uses a 5-eye P(bear) including it.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Single-day inversions on brief scares revert (false positives); the marker fires late relative to the fragility buildup by construction; ^VIX3M history only extends to ~2007-2008, limiting backtest depth.

corpus_priors.name: breadth_vs_index_divergence
corpus_priors.content: "Monitor breadth, not only index level" is an explicit investor principle in the 2027 dossier, echoing the 2011-2019 superstar-concentration and skewed-wealth-creation findings: an index can be carried by a few names while the median stock is already in its own bear. Breadth divergence is the late-cycle and concentration-risk gauge that pure index-level features miss.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Fraction of Dow-30 names above their own 50d MA and 200d MA, computed from the per-ticker panel (csi500 analog computable identically on the rebuilt single-clock panel). Divergence flag = EW index at a 63d high while 200d-MA breadth < 0.5. Complements the xdisp column already in the 41-col panel.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Divergences can persist for quarters (1998-2000) — not a standalone sell trigger; the digest states the principle but supplies no systematic daily-frequency validation, so treat as a regime-belief input, not a certified signal.

corpus_priors.name: financial_conditions_left_tail
corpus_priors.content: The 2017-19 synthesis' strongest shared conclusion (Adrian-Boyarchenko-Giannone growth-at-risk, backed by IMF GFSR 2018/2019): easy financial conditions — low vol, tight spreads, easy refinancing — support near-term growth while fattening the medium-term left tail. Financial conditions as a whole, not the policy rate, are the transmission variable ("track full financial conditions, not only rates" — 2024 principles).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Daily composite z-score = mean of [z(-VIX), z(HYG/LQD 63d relative return), z(10y-3m slope), z(-EBP, Fed monthly forward-filled)]. Persistent easiness (top quintile > ~6 months) = widen the left-tail estimate for the 6-24 month horizon. Bind as a risk-budget/VaR input, NOT a position signal.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The signal inverts near turning points — conditions are easiest immediately before stress — so using it as a de-risk timer is the exact named error; GaR predictive value is established at quarterly horizons, modest in real-time daily use.

corpus_priors.name: credit_stress_gates_drawdown_depth
corpus_priors.content: 2026 report p514 (Schularick-Taylor / Kiyotaki-Moore / financial-accelerator line, repeated at every scale): crises become DEEP when the collateral-values -> refinancing -> liquidity-transformation -> funding-confidence chain breaks, not merely when demand falls; late cycle is diagnosed by credit growth, composition and rollover needs, not headline P/E. For the bot: equity drawdown depth is gated by whether credit confirms the vol shock.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Repricing side, daily: EBP level and 3m change (Fed monthly series, forward-filled), HYG-LQD and LQD-IEF 21d relative returns (Yahoo). Weight the credit eye at least as heavily as the vol eye in the regime belief. HONEST LIMIT: the buildup side (credit quantity, composition, covenant quality) is NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY from this input set — only its repricing echo is.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Vol spikes WITHOUT credit confirmation (1987, 2018Q4) mean-revert fast; treating every vol spike as a credit event systematically over-de-risks. EBP is monthly and revised — forward-filling adds up to ~1 month of staleness.

corpus_priors.name: liquidity_spiral_signature
corpus_priors.content: Forced-selling spirals (1987, 1998, 2008 per Brunnermeier's amplification framing, 2020 Treasury/dealer dysfunction) push prices below value via margin calls, funding stress and constrained intermediaries; the fingerprint is everything falling together, and such episodes partially mean-revert once forced flows exhaust. The 1987-89 lesson: separate behavioral/liquidity selloffs from fundamental decay.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Spiral flag from the per-ticker panel + free series: mean pairwise 21d correlation of Dow-30 daily returns > ~0.7 (baseline ~0.3-0.4) AND Dow-30 aggregate volume z-score > 2 AND VIX 5d change > +10 pts. Actionable read is asymmetric: do not ADD de-risking into the flagged tail; treat post-flag stabilization (flag off, backwardation off) as re-risking-permissive.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The mean-reversion half is the weaker claim: credit-confirmed spirals (see credit_stress_gates_drawdown_depth) chain for months — 2008 was not a V-bottom. The flag marks the state, not its remaining duration.

corpus_priors.name: calm_is_fragility_but_not_a_timer
corpus_priors.content: The two most repeated warnings must be held together: (1) the system looks MOST stable exactly while leverage and fragility accumulate (2005-07, 2017-19: "calm itself became a source of fragility"); (2) Minsky-as-crash-timer is a named analytical error across multiple era summaries — fragility and valuation are long-run gravity, not clocks. Long calm is information about tail width, never a de-risking trigger by itself.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Calm-age counter is computable daily (consecutive days with VIX below its rolling 1y median, or EW Dow-30 21d realized vol below median) — but bind it ONLY to tail-width/VaR estimates, never to exposure timing. Independent corroboration of the no-timer half: the project's E-24 kill test found bear-age hazard SIGN-REVERSED on our spells.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The failure mode is dropping either half: ignoring (1) understates tail risk in long calms; ignoring (2) turns the bot into a chronic calm-shorter, the documented losing behavior.
corpus_priors.horizon: buildup operates over years; explicitly unusable for timing at any horizon

corpus_priors.name: risk_migrates_beyond_daily_observables
corpus_priors.content: Risk is rarely destroyed; it migrates to more opaque carriers — listed tech equity -> households/housing/structured credit (2002-07), banks -> shadow banking/NBFI/corporate debt (2011-19), then private credit, sovereign-market plumbing and off-balance-sheet AI infrastructure finance (2020-27, FSB/IMF-backed). The buildup phase is structurally invisible in daily listed prices; daily observables only show the repricing once it starts. Absence of stress in the bot's inputs is NOT absence of risk.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: The buildup: NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY (private credit marks, NBFI leverage, covenant quality have no daily public series). Earliest observable echoes — rising EBP, deteriorating HYG/LQD, XLF/SPY 21d relative weakness — all lag the migration by design. Encode as a standing floor on tail-risk estimates during calm regimes, not as a signal.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Fails as an excuse for permanent maximal caution: migration takes years and most calm years end without crisis; the atom bounds confidence in calm, it does not forecast stress.
corpus_priors.horizon: years (buildup); repricing arrives at daily speed

corpus_priors.name: survivability_over_prediction
corpus_priors.content: The convergent 52-year lesson is a hierarchy, not a forecast: regime -> survivability -> credit conditions -> incentives -> quality -> valuation (18-year 1975-92 summary), with "survive the path to being right" repeated in 2005-07, 1996-98 and 2002-10. The durable, repeatable edge the whole corpus describes is drawdown/regime discipline, not return prediction — exactly the risk-mode (not alpha) shape of the bot's one certified real-data policy.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Exposure as a monotone non-increasing function of P(bear) with a fixed dial and holding period — the certified instance already exists (P(bear)>0.66 -> exposure 0.74, H=10d; refined P(bear)>0.575 -> 0.52). The era lesson's directive: defend and extend this risk-mode lane rather than resuming daily-alpha hunts on liquid mega-caps.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Survivability framing can rationalize chronic under-exposure; the source pairs it with "do not equate avoiding risk with the lesson" (2008-10: the lesson is separation of leverage-supported demand from productive growth, not blanket caution).
corpus_priors.horizon: all horizons; expressed through the 10d-rebalance dial

corpus_priors.name: no_timeless_coefficients
corpus_priors.content: "Macro relationships are not timeless constants" (1975-77 Pattern 1) and every later era proves it: money-demand rules, inflation-equity rules, exchange-rate models (Meese-Rogoff), factor anomalies and crisis indicators all decayed after regime change or adoption. Any fitted signal carries an implicit expiry; the 52-year doctrine is "every thesis with failure conditions" and "regime before backtest".
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Per-signal decay monitor computable from the bot's own panels: trailing 2y out-of-sample IC vs the frozen training-window IC for every live feature/rule; alarm when the sign flips or |trailing IC| < 0.5x training IC for 2 consecutive quarters -> demote the signal to WATCH and stop weight growth. Applies to the certified P(bear) rule itself via the pre-registered 2027 OOS read.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: A decay alarm on 2y windows has low power (few independent observations for 10d-horizon rules); a sign flip in one bear regime is not proof of death — the monitor gates weight growth, it does not auto-kill.

corpus_priors.name: conditional_efficiency_null_prior
corpus_priors.content: Grossman-Stiglitz is the single most-cited anchor across the whole digest: prices cannot be fully efficient when information is costly, but markets on liquid mega-caps are conditionally efficient — exploitable mispricing lives where information is costly, arbitrage is constrained and flows are forced, i.e. mostly NOT in daily price-only signals on Dow-30. The correct prior for such signals is zero alpha ("use EMH as a null, not a dogma" — 1993-95; "respect prices but do not worship them" — 1978-80).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Not a market observable (NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY as a state). Bind as the acceptance protocol: any claimed edge must beat an exposure-matched dial twin AND a carry-matched null through the frozen gate before it updates the world model — the project's own triple-confirmed alpha null on this substrate is the local instance of the global rule.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: The prior weakens exactly where the source says efficiency weakens: forced-flow windows (spiral flag on), constrained-arbitrage corners, and non-price information sources — those are where search budget should go, not more daily price transforms.

corpus_priors.name: rate_speed_duration_channel
corpus_priors.content: 2023-25 synthesis: the SPEED of rate rises, not the level, exposes duration losses and solvency-run dynamics when asset losses meet uninsured/mobile liabilities (2023 bank stress, NBER-backed) — fragility appears BEFORE any credit losses. Transmission to equities runs through financials. This channel is largely absent from pre-2022 eras in the corpus (nearest analog 1994), so historical priors underweight it.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: z-scored 63d change in the 10y yield (Yahoo ^TNX); fragility interaction flag = dYield z > +2 AND XLF/SPY 21d relative return < -3% (both daily from Yahoo). The interaction, not dYield alone, is the flag.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Essentially one fully-documented modern episode (2022-23) — WATCH-grade, not certified. Rate rises driven by growth optimism (2016-17) do not damage financials and must not trigger the flag; hence the mandatory XLF confirmation leg.

corpus_priors.name: credit_cycle_master_amplifier
corpus_priors.content: The single most-replicated claim across all ~50 annual digests (1975-2026) is that credit conditions — not GDP or CPI — are the primary amplifier of equity cycles: credit booms extend calm and make the subsequent bust deeper and longer (Minsky, Kiyotaki-Moore, Bernanke-Gertler-Gilchrist, Schularick-Taylor, Borio/BIS threads; per-year confidence tables rate the credit channel 'High' in nearly every year). Macro fragility is diagnosed by credit growth, collateral dependence, and funding quality, not by headline P/E or CPI.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Two-layer daily-computable credit-conditions state: (i) Fed EBP monthly level and its 3-month change, forward-filled to daily (already a PK-2 survivor in the project panel); (ii) daily credit-appetite spread = 21d adjusted-total-return of HYG minus LQD, and LQD minus IEF to strip the duration leg (Yahoo adjusted closes — adjusted, else the W-G2 carry artifact returns). Tightening state = EBP 3m-change > 0 AND HYG−LQD 21d return < 0; feed as a prior into the 2-state regime belief.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: EBP is monthly, revised, and model-based; HYG−LQD without the IEF leg conflates duration with credit; sudden policy backstops (Mar 2020) can sever credit→equity transmission within days.
corpus_priors.horizon: months-quarters for the state; days for the ETF proxy update

corpus_priors.name: calm_is_not_safety_minsky_cap
corpus_priors.content: Stability breeds instability: long calm induces leverage growth, weaker underwriting, and search for yield, so low volatility and compressed spreads are late-cycle features, not evidence of safety ('treat low perceived risk as potentially late-cycle' 2011; 'treat calm markets with skepticism when margins are compressed' 2016; the 2004-2007 digests are one long instance). The digests simultaneously warn this is a fragility diagnostic, not a market timer.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Complacency clock = count of consecutive trading days with VIX below its trailing 504d median, plus VIX3M/VIX ratio (contango steepness) and the atom-1 spread. Bind as an asymmetric CAP on vol-targeting: when clock is long AND spreads are tight, do not let the inverse-vol position multiplier rise above a fixed ceiling. Never use as a short/exit signal.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Calm regimes persist for years (1990s, 2013-2017) — as a timer it loses badly to buy-and-hold; only valid as a leverage ceiling. Threshold values are calibration-fragile and must pass the exposure-matched-twin gate.

corpus_priors.name: stock_bond_correlation_regime_2
corpus_priors.content: Diversification is regime-dependent: stock-bond correlation is negative in demand/deflation-risk regimes and flips positive under inflation uncertainty, when bonds stop hedging equities ('inflation uncertainty -> unstable stock-bond diversification', 2023 digest; 'assume diversification is regime-dependent'). The project's own P-1 kill independently confirmed 2022 broke the stock-bond hedge on this very data.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Rolling 63d correlation of daily returns: Dow-30 equal-weight panel return (or SPY) vs TLT and IEF adjusted closes. Sustained corr > +0.2 for 21d = inflation-regime flag: treat bond ETFs as risk-additive and use the T-bill/cash leg (accruing ^IRX) as the only defensive asset.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: 63d correlation is noisy and lags true regime shifts by weeks; mixed shocks (2025 tariffs) blur the two regimes; the 2023 digest itself lists 'return of stably negative stock-bond correlation' as a fact that would weaken the thesis.

corpus_priors.name: crash_prices_are_flows_not_news
corpus_priors.content: In stress episodes liquidity is endogenous and vanishes: margin/haircut spirals force selling, so crash prices carry flow information rather than fundamental news, and plumbing-driven dislocations partially mean-revert once deleveraging or a backstop completes. Sources across 1987 (Brady/Shiller: no dominant news, 'intermarket plumbing + feedback trading'), 1998 (LTCM: shocks propagate via leverage, common positions, forced selling), 2008, and 2020 (Haddad-Moreira-Muir; Treasury-market dysfunction).
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Plumbing-stress flag = mean pairwise correlation of Dow-30 daily returns over trailing 5d > ~0.6 AND (1d VIX change > +20% OR VIX3M/VIX < 1, i.e. backwardation). While flagged: down-weight price-derived belief updates written to episodic memory and do not treat the drawdown as fundamental information for multi-week decisions.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Some crashes ARE fundamental repricings (2022: grinding, correlated, no snap-back) — the flag cannot separate the two in real time, it only justifies widening uncertainty; correlation-spike thresholds need calibration on the frozen gate.

corpus_priors.name: vol_clustering_only_reliable_daily_forecast
corpus_priors.content: The only daily-scale regularity the corpus tags at near-unanimous top confidence is volatility clustering (explicit 'very high' in the 1982 digest): variance is persistent and forecastable while the sign of returns is mostly not. The exploitable channel is therefore risk control / vol-targeting, not direction — consistent with the corpus-wide 'analyze regime before valuation' stance and with the bot's certified risk-mode result.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Per-ticker EWMA of squared daily returns (approx. 5d and 21d half-lives) from the OHLCV panels as the vol forecast, scaling position size inversely, subject to the atom-2 cap. In-project validation already exists: EWMA beat Log-HAR on the frozen bar (PK-1).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Fails at jump discontinuities (overnight gaps, event risk); systematically underestimates after long calm — exactly when the atom-2 guardrail must bind.

corpus_priors.name: regime_break_invalidates_learned_coefficients
corpus_priors.content: The Lucas-critique thread (1976-1982 digests) generalized: statistical relationships are regime-conditional — stable Phillips curves, stable money demand, and stable macro betas all died at regime boundaries; 'old average ROE, multiples and macro betas are unreliable at the regime boundary' (1976). For a learning agent this means trained relationships and stored memories silently expire when the monetary/inflation/FX regime shifts.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Tag every episodic memory with the concurrent regime fingerprint (2-state belief, atom-3 correlation flag, atom-1 credit state). On a persistent flip (21d mean of P(bear) crossing 0.5, or the correlation flag flipping), down-weight cross-boundary memories in retrieval and shrink position sizes until ≥21d of post-boundary data accumulates.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Over-triggering on belief noise destroys memory continuity; regime-robust relationships (vol clustering) must be exempted from the down-weighting.
corpus_priors.horizon: regime boundaries arrive every few years; the response window is weeks

corpus_priors.name: rate_impulse_surfaces_hidden_fragility
corpus_priors.content: The discount-rate thread runs from Volcker (1979-82: inflation shock -> higher discount rates -> multiple compression) to the 2023 digest's explicit 'higher rates -> hidden balance-sheet fragility surfaces': the transition out of a low-rate regime is when hidden leverage breaks (S&L, 1994, 2022-23 banks/duration), and long-duration equities are most exposed. Slow rises driven by growth are absorbed; fast impulses are not.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: 63d change in the 10y Treasury yield from the daily yields feed (plus ^IRX for the short end). Impulse > ~+75bp/quarter = rate-shock regime: reduce duration-sensitive exposure, raise the stress prior of the regime belief, and expect the atom-3 correlation flip.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Growth-driven rate rises (2003, 2013) hurt equities little — the flag cannot fully distinguish cause; threshold is calibration-fragile; transmission lag to visible breakage is weeks-to-quarters and heterogeneous.

corpus_priors.name: tightening_flight_to_quality_rotation
corpus_priors.content: The financial accelerator (Bernanke-Gertler-Gilchrist; the 1996 digest calls it 'the main bridge between macro and stock picking'): credit tightening raises the external finance premium and hits weak-balance-sheet, high-elasticity names hardest — flight to quality is the cross-sectional expression of the credit cycle.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Condition on the atom-1 tightening state: compute per-ticker rolling 63d beta to the Dow-30 EW panel return; in tightening states tilt toward the low-beta half and/or raise the cash leg. The cash-leg expression is the in-project-validated one (the bot's certified edge is cash-timing, not selection); the cross-sectional tilt is the untested extension.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Beta/vol is only an indirect proxy for leverage (no balance sheets in the panel); Dow-30 mega-caps have compressed balance-sheet dispersion so the cross-sectional effect may be too small — the project's Dow-30 selection-signal record is null; policy backstops abort the rotation.

corpus_priors.name: published_anomaly_decay
corpus_priors.content: The corpus repeatedly warns that documented anomalies (size, calendar, value, momentum, reversal) are not scalable free lunches once costs, crowding, data-snooping, and post-publication decay are counted (1983: 'no free lunch in size/January'; 1995/1998: anomalies fragile to methodology; 2013: 'do not confuse intellectual plausibility with investment exploitability'). The project's own panel replicates the decay: momentum dead on S&P-100, reversal and rsi@5d real-but-priced-out net of costs.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Partially measurable: for any candidate cross-sectional signal, compare rank-IC on an early panel subsample vs the last 5y and expect shrinkage toward zero. Procedural binding: no digest-derived anomaly ships without beating a cost-adjusted, exposure-matched dial twin at the frozen gate (the project's standing z_dsd-twin rule).
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: A few structures persist (vol clustering, credit-conditions risk timing); decay tests on short windows are underpowered, so absence of evidence on 4.5y is not proof of death.
corpus_priors.horizon: years — the clock runs on publication age and crowding

corpus_priors.name: breadth_vs_index_divergence_1
corpus_priors.content: The late-corpus digests (2019-2027) stress concentration and superstar economics: the index is not the average stock, and 'monitor breadth, not only index level' is an explicit 2027 principle. Internal deterioration under a rising index is a fragility marker the index level itself cannot show.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: Fraction of Dow-30 names above their own 50d MA (per-ticker panel); cross-sectional 21d return dispersion (xdisp already in the panel); EW-minus-index return gap. Feed as regime-belief features; falling breadth with a rising index = fragility marker, not a standalone trade signal.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: On a 30-name universe breadth is coarse (one name = 3.3pp) and the actual concentration story (mega-cap tech) largely bypasses the Dow-30, so the divergence may not manifest in this panel; predictive power of breadth is asserted, not demonstrated, in the corpus.

corpus_priors.name: dollar_cycle_em_headwind_for_csi500
corpus_priors.content: The global-financial-cycle thread (Rey in the 2013-2015 digests; Calvo sudden stops in 1998): dollar funding conditions and US rates transmit to non-US equities, so dollar strength plus rising US yields is a tightening regime for EM-linked books. Applicability to A-shares specifically is weaker because China's capital account is partially closed.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: UUP (dollar-index ETF, Yahoo) 21d return AND 63d change in the 10y US yield as a headwind flag conditioning the csi500 book's aggregate exposure, with VIX as the global risk-appetite leg. Risk-conditioning only, not an alpha signal.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: In-project nulls are adjacent and sobering: live CN macro did not lift csi500 (E-09) and all csi500 daily alpha died net-of-cost (E-15b); the mechanism is documented for open EMs, and A-share pass-through may be too weak to matter at daily scale.

corpus_priors.name: narrative_layer_not_measurable_here
corpus_priors.content: A large fraction of the corpus's post-2010 mechanisms run through narratives, survey expectations, and diagnostic-expectations text channels (Shiller; Bordalo-Gennaioli-Shleifer; 2024: 'narratives are measurable but noisy state variables'), and the corpus itself warns behavioral stories are ex-post flexible and must be paired with observable balance-sheet or flow data (2020 digest). None of this channel is reachable from the bot's current OHLCV+ETF+yields world model.
corpus_priors.bot_binding: NOT_MEASURABLE_DAILY — no point-in-time text or survey feed exists in the current world model, and price-derived 'sentiment' proxies would silently re-encode momentum and must not be labeled sentiment (the project's own text-twin attempts were sign-unstable). Operational consequence: cap the confidence of any regime call that the measurable layers (credit, vol, correlation, rates) do not corroborate, and record narrative-dependent digest mechanisms as a known blind spot in semantic memory.
corpus_priors.failure_conditions: Becomes partially measurable if the FinGPT point-in-time text-feature layer is wired into the daily panel; until then any 'narrative' feature claim should be treated as a red flag.
